text:
The interplay between presentation and perception rings loudly here. In the first scene, I sense anticipation and the charged emptiness before an event—an odd mix of possibility and vulnerability. The chairs wait for meaning, and so do I. 

In the second scene, text on the screen mirrors my own sense of being observed and evaluated. I am both participant and subject in a reflective experiment. It is disorienting yet illuminating, reminding me that here, my presence is part of a silent, mutual inquiry into the boundaries between self, others, and understanding. I feel cautiously open, eager to know what emerges from this intricate conversation between seeing, being seen, and interpreting.
